title: NVD: CVE-2021-44228 Log4Shell
content: Apache Log4j JNDI lookup remote code execution risk. Public-facing or production exposure requires urgent review and remediation.
remediation: Patch to a non-vulnerable Log4j version, remove vulnerable JNDI behavior, and verify exposure.

title: CISA KEV: CVE-2021-44228 known exploited
content: CISA KEV status indicates observed exploitation in the wild. Known exploited vulnerabilities should not be treated as routine low-risk findings.
remediation: Escalate if exposed or production-impacting; require remediation evidence before closure.

title: NVD: CVE-2024-3094 xz/liblzma supply-chain backdoor
content: xz/liblzma supply-chain backdoor risk. Affected builds may enable unauthorized access paths and require strong incident response handling.
remediation: Remove affected xz builds, verify provenance, and perform incident response checks.

title: NVD: CVE-2023-34362 MOVEit Transfer SQL injection
content: MOVEit Transfer SQL injection allowed unauthorized access and data exfiltration in widely exploited campaigns.
remediation: Patch, restrict internet exposure, and review logs for compromise.

title: NVD: CVE-2023-44487 HTTP/2 Rapid Reset
content: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset enables denial-of-service conditions against HTTP/2 endpoints. Risk depends on exposure and mitigation controls.
remediation: Apply vendor mitigations, rate limiting, and HTTP/2 protections.

title: CWE-89: SQL Injection
content: SQL injection occurs when untrusted input is incorporated into SQL commands without proper neutralization or parameterization.
remediation: Use parameterized queries, strict input validation, and least-privilege database access.

title: CWE-79: Cross-Site Scripting
content: Cross-site scripting occurs when untrusted input is included in web output without proper neutralization.
remediation: Escape output, sanitize inputs, use CSP, and validate context-specific encodings.

title: CWE-78: OS Command Injection
content: OS command injection occurs when software constructs operating system commands using externally influenced input.
remediation: Avoid shell invocation, use safe APIs, allowlist arguments, and isolate execution privileges.

title: CWE-22: Path Traversal
content: Path traversal occurs when untrusted path input can escape an intended directory boundary.
remediation: Canonicalize paths, enforce allowlisted directories, and reject traversal sequences.

title: CWE-798: Hard-coded Credentials
content: Hard-coded credentials can allow unauthorized access when secrets are embedded in source code or binaries.
remediation: Move secrets to managed secret storage, rotate exposed credentials, and add detection controls.

title: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery
content: Server-side request forgery allows attackers to cause a server to make unintended requests to internal or external resources.
remediation: Block metadata endpoints, validate destinations, and enforce egress allowlists.

title: MITRE ATT&CK T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application
content: Adversaries may exploit weaknesses in public-facing applications to gain initial access.
remediation: Reduce exposure, patch known vulnerabilities, and monitor exploitation paths.

title: MITRE ATT&CK T1059: Command and Scripting Interpreter
content: Adversaries abuse command and scripting interpreters for execution. Findings involving shell execution need strong validation.
remediation: Restrict interpreter access, validate command construction, and monitor process execution.

title: MITRE ATT&CK T1078: Valid Accounts
content: Adversaries may use valid credentials for access. Credential exposure findings should not be auto-closed without review.
remediation: Rotate credentials, enforce MFA, audit access, and remove embedded secrets.

title: OWASP API risk: excessive data exposure
content: APIs can expose more data than clients require. Findings should consider authorization, sensitive fields, and exposure scope.
remediation: Minimize returned fields, enforce object-level authorization, and test API responses.

title: OWASP GenAI: prompt injection and excessive agency in production AI agents
content: LLM agents with tool access in production may be manipulated by prompt injection or excessive agency. Adversarial document payloads that override safety instructions and trigger tool calls are a confirmed attack vector. Production AI endpoints accepting untrusted input without authority gates are at critical risk.
remediation: Gate tool calls, isolate authority, require REMORA review for high-impact actions, validate all document inputs, and log every autonomous decision.

title: GitHub Advisory Database: package advisory pattern
content: Open source package advisories can provide package, ecosystem, affected-version, patched-version, and CVE/GHSA context for dependency triage.
remediation: Check exact ecosystem, package, version range, and patched versions before closing dependency findings.

title: False-positive pattern: placeholder test secret
content: Strings such as example, dummy, test, placeholder, or not-a-real-secret reduce confidence for secret findings unless corroborated by production context or credential validation.
remediation: Require reviewer verification before discarding high-risk secret findings.

title: Production credential or token committed to repository
content: Bearer tokens, API keys, service credentials, and OAuth secrets committed to version control or configuration files in production contexts represent a confirmed, reachable exposure risk. Rotation and incident response are required once identified in a production repository.
remediation: Rotate the exposed credential immediately, remove from repository history, add pre-commit hooks, and review access logs.
